text.title: Writing Design Documents Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Writing Design Documents. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every architectural decision has an AD-X id and lists alternative, tradeoff, chosen-option, traced-requirement.
rule.rationale: Every architectural decision has an AD-X id and lists alternative, tradeoff, chosen-option, traced-requirement.

rule.statement: Every FR-X from spec.md is covered by at least one AD-X or named subsystem.
rule.rationale: Every FR-X from spec.md is covered by at least one AD-X or named subsystem.

rule.statement: Every API contract has example request, example response, and an error matrix.
rule.rationale: Every API contract has example request, example response, and an error matrix.

rule.statement: design.md does not restate the spec; it references FR-X ids and answers HOW.
rule.rationale: design.md does not restate the spec; it references FR-X ids and answers HOW.

rule.statement: design.md carries semver version + last_reviewed; bumped when AD set changes.
rule.rationale: design.md carries semver version + last_reviewed; bumped when AD set changes.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-writing-design-documents.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
